behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification behavior on explicit content. A mocked classifier can hide true misclassification risk and would overstate reliability.
behaviors.test_description: Run a test against the real vision moderation model using a curated explicit-image set. Assert each sample is flagged, routed to hold-for-review state, and not published. Verify hold/publish side effects in storage/queue records.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior validates robustness of real model semantics under adversarial text variants, not just routing logic. Mocking text classification would likely miss the core failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a corpus of real obfuscated hate-speech variants to the production-like text moderation model endpoint. Assert they are flagged and held for review. Include unicode confusables and leetspeak variants to expose model robustness gaps.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The key risk is decision wiring across modules (image result + text result + publish path), including state transitions and persistence. This is primarily a module interaction boundary, not model-quality validation.
behaviors.test_description: In an integration test, wire moderation orchestrator, decision engine, and publish/storage modules with deterministic pass results from classifier adapters. Assert final state is published, no hold record exists, and no drop/loss occurs in queues or DB.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end latency SLO that depends on real service runtime behavior (network calls, process scheduling, queueing, I/O). Pure integration mocks can hide real timing failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end system performance test in a production-like environment with real service processes and real external call paths (or realistic networked dependencies). Measure p95/p99 submission-to-decision latency and assert the 5-second requirement under peak-hour load profile.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is fallback behavior at the boundary between model client, retry queue, and decision logic. This can be caught by integrated components with controlled model-unavailable errors; full OS/network realism is helpful but not strictly minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Run an integration test with real orchestrator + queue + decision modules and a vision client adapter that returns timeout/503/unavailable errors. Assert image posts are enqueued with retry metadata and never published/auto-approved during outage handling.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency on borderline content depends on real model variability and/or stochastic scoring behavior. A mocked deterministic classifier would likely miss instability that appears in real inference.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly to the real vision moderation path within the target window, recording raw scores/labels and final decisions. Assert decision consistency constraints (or bounded variance policy) and verify no oscillation between publish/hold states.
